response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with genuine developer-tooling credentials — he worked on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments and has authored test frameworks and libraries — but his career centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang rather than the JVM. The role's hard requirements of strong Java and Kotlin expertise, deep JVM understanding, and solid Spring ecosystem experience are essentially unmet: Java appears only briefly in side contexts, and Kotlin, Spring, Quarkus, and Hibernate do not appear at all. Strong engineer, wrong stack for this position.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Authentic language-tooling interest: built test runners, assertion libraries, and web frameworks from scratch
Broad senior-level experience across high-throughput backend systems, TDD, and CI/CD practices
concerns: No demonstrable Kotlin experience and only marginal Java exposure
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM framework background
IDE work dates to 2014-2015 and was in the C ecosystem, not JVM tooling

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
